notes: One; phonemes o (short) and oo (long). Both mean 1 in Tamil.
Three; phonemes mu (short) and muu (long). Seal 2097: muu-da = muuththa (aged). Seal 2167: muu-ya = oxen.
Four; phonemes na (short) and naa (long). Key for ghee: na+y = ney (ghee). Seals: 2950=nanku y=ghee; 1076=thava thava na y=very good ghee; 1133=na y=ney (final evolved form).
Six also means 'river' (aaRu) in Tamil. Seal 1007: aaRu ya = one who has a river.
Seven; phonemes e (short) and ee (long). Seal 2478: mi mi eezu njan aa = very good seven young cows.
Eight; phoneme eN. Also means 'sesame' (eNNai=sesame oil) in Tamil.
Twelve. Seal 1414: iLa vya muu panniru aa = young 3x12 (36) cows. Highest seen: 3x12=36 cows.
